mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs within the next trading week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances following a 0.2pp UNRATE increase.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of accommodative monetary policy reflects expanding real output capacity that supports corporate earnings and generates positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during easing cycles yield negative or flat 20-day forward returns in over half of the sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding trend levels signals robust aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates above 2.5% annualized fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns with statistical significance over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in over 50% of months following CPIAUCSL increases above 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real personal spending during late-cycle expansions signals weakening consumer demand that precedes immediate equity market corrections within the next trading week.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines in late-cycle regimes fail to predict negative 5-day forward returns, with positive returns occurring in over 60% of cases.
